summary: Mnemosyne is a multi-bank memory system for AI agents, providing persistent memory with hybrid retrieval (vector, graph, fact, temporal). 916 nodes across 206 files, 1356 edges. Core is in ~45K lines of Python.

layers.name: Core Engine
layers.description: Memory banks, hybrid recall, embeddings, annotations, triples, beam (sleep/dream pipeline), conflict detection, query pipeline (cache, intent, synonyms, temporal parsing, weibull decay), content sanitization, MMR re-ranking, orchestrator

layers.name: Extraction & Diagnostics
layers.description: LLM-based fact extraction from user messages, tiered diagnostics (C13.b observability layer), OpenRouter multi-model client with fallback and retry

layers.name: Memory Importers
layers.description: 8 provider importers (Cognee, Hindsight, Honcho, Letta, Mem0, SuperMemory, Zep) plus AgenticImporter for script/document generation. All extend BaseImporter except AgenticImporter

layers.name: Hermes Provider (Plugin)
layers.description: Hermes Agent memory provider plugin. MnemosyneMemoryProvider with 55+ methods, 20+ tool schemas, audit log, thread isolation, shared-surface support, LLM adapter

layers.name: CLI & Operations
layers.description: CLI commands, diagnostics, install, recovery backup, 5 maintenance scripts (noise cleanup, legacy migration, LLM quality healing, memoria audit, stats dashboard), 5 benchmark tools (audit log, unified recall, validation, BEAM charts, SOTA report)

layers.name: Hermes Integration (pip install)
layers.description: Version-tracked Hermes plugin integration with audited CLI, LLM adapter, installer. v0.1.1 vs provider's v3.1.0 — denotes packaging duality

layers.name: VS Code Extension
layers.description: 4-TypeScript-file VS Code extension: client (Python subprocess bridge), extension entry (4 commands), provider (sidebar WebviewView), webview (detail panel)

layers.name: Obsidian Plugin
layers.description: Obsidian plugin for memory browsing and search, TypeScript, esbuild build, 6 features (sidebar, search, store, stats, auto-refresh)

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: CI matrix (Python 3.10-3.13), PyPI release workflow via Trusted Publishing, Docker multi-stage build for Railway, pre-commit PII blocker, pre-push SemVer tag validator

architectureInsights: Hierarchical bank architecture: short-term → working → archival → consolidated, with typed memory classification (13 types, zero-LLM)
Polyphonic recall: 4 parallel retrieval voices (vector, graph, fact, temporal) with RRF re-ranking
BEAM (Bayesian Episodic Active Memory): sleep/dream cycle for memory consolidation and conflict detection
Two-tier conflict detection: heuristic base tier + LLM-powered tier (gated via MNEMOSYNE_LLM_CONFLICT_DETECTION env var)
Veracity system: tracks confidence levels across extraction sources, consolidates conflicting facts
Binary vector compression (MIB): 32x compression ratio for memory embeddings
Dual packaging: direct provider plugin (v3.1.0) vs pip-installable integration (v0.1.1)
Query pipeline: intent classification → synonym expansion → cache tier (5 tiers) → temporal decay (Weibull per memory type)
C13.b extraction diagnostics: process-global thread-safe observability for LLM extraction failures that were previously silent
coverageGaps: 4 import edge batches (10, 11, 14, 17, 19, 20) had malformed output — some subagents wrote analysis-only JSON instead of node/edge format
175 nodes defaulted to moderate complexity (None assigned by analyzer)
18 unknown node types (data) — mostly JSON fixtures and test data files
Missing and planned docs: 11 of 17 doc files in batch 15 don't exist on disk
orchestrator.py is a stub/placeholder with no real recall logic
